metadata.name: Leyline of the Void — if in opening hand, enters at start of game
metadata.description: Leyline of the Void can be in play at the start of the game if it was in the opening hand. If not, it can be cast for {2}{B}{B}. This script tests casting Leyline for its mana cost mid-game. When it enters the battlefield, it establishes a replacement effect: cards put into opponents' graveyards are instead exiled.
metadata.generation_notes: Leyline of the Void oracle: 'If Leyline of the Void is in your opening hand, you may begin the game with it on the battlefield. If a card that an opponent owns would be put into a graveyard from anywhere, exile it instead.' This script tests casting it normally for {2}{B}{B}. After it enters, any card an opponent owns that would go to graveyard is exiled. Then p2's creature dies (Llanowar Elves via a Lightning Bolt on p1) — but wait, it's p2 who owns Llanowar Elves. When it would go to p2's graveyard, instead it goes to exile.

script.step_note: p1 casts Leyline of the Void for its mana cost.

actions.description: Initial: Leyline of the Void in hand

actions.note: CR 601.2: p1 casts Leyline of the Void; pays {2}{B}{B}.
Both pass — Leyline of the Void resolves.

resolution.note: Leyline of the Void enters battlefield.

resolution.description: If a card that p2 owns would be put into a graveyard from anywhere, exile it instead.

actions.note: Leyline enters battlefield; replacement effect registered.

actions.description: After Leyline enters: on battlefield, replacement effect active
actions.note: Leyline of the Void on battlefield. Graveyard replacement effect active for p2's cards.
